description: The person or people to whom the #Speaker is directing the reading of the #Text.

name: Speaker
description: The peronr who reads the #Text aloud.

name: Text
description: Tthe source from which the #Speaker reads.

name: Manner
description: The way that the #Speaker reads the #Text aloud.

name: Time
description: When the reading takes place.

name: Purpose
description: The result that the #Speaker is trying to achieve by reading aloud.

name: Place
description: The location at which the reading takes place.

name: Means
description:
The method in which the #Text is read aloud by the #Speaker.
<ex>My mother always <t>read</t> to me <fex name="Means">by candle light</fex>.</ex>

name: Reader
description: The one who examines a Text to understand it.

name: Text
description: The entity that contains linguistic symbols.

name: Manner
description: Manner of performing an action

name: Means
description:
An act that allows the #Reader to read in the specified manner.
<ex>I <t>skimmed</t> the book <fex name="Mns">by reading just the section headings and the first sentences after</fex>.</ex>

name: Purpose
description: The #Purpose is the reason for which the #Reader reads the #Text.

name: Time
description:
When the reading event takes place.
<ex>Charlie often <t>reads</t> the newspaper <fex name="Time">at night</fex>.</ex>

name: Place
description:
Where the reading event takes place.
<ex>Surprisingly, few of the students <t>read</t> much at all <fex name="Place">in the designated reading rooms</fex>.</ex>

name: Context
description: The context wherein the #Reader reads a particular #Text.

name: Agent
description:
The person who responds to a #State_of_Affairs by performing some #Action.

<ex>Those are <fex name="Agent">my</fex> <t>reasons</t> , " said Toby . </ex>

name: Action
description:
The action that the #Agent performs in response to a #State_of_Affairs.

<ex> I find that all my <t>reasons</t> <fex name="Action">for postponing giving birth</fex> still apply </ex>

name: State_of_affairs
description:
The eventuality  that motivates the #Agent"s performing a particular #Action in response to it.

<ex>  My <t>reason</t> <m>is</m> <fex name="State_of_affairs">that our cities are now designed round the motor car</fex></ex>

name: Content
description: The #Arguer wants the #Addressee to entertain the #Content, which can be a proposition to be believed or a course of action to be taken, based on the #Support presented.<ex>Jan <t>argued</t> <fex name="Content">that the evidence was inadmissable</fex>.</ex>

name: Arguer
description: The #Arguer presents the #Content before the #Addressee.<ex><fex name="Arguer">Jan</fex> <t>argued</t> that the evidence was inadmissable.</ex>

name: Medium
description: This FE identifies the physical or abstract setting in which the #Content is conveyed.<ex>Jan <t>argued</t> <fex name="Medium">in writing</fex> that the evidence was inadmissable.</ex>

name: Addressee
description: #Addressee is the entity before whom the #Arguer presents a #Content. <ex>Jan <t>argued</t> <fex name="Addressee">to the court</fex> that the evidence was inadmissable.</ex>

name: Support
description: #Support lends epistemic support to a claim, or provides a reason for a course of action.<ex>Jan <t>argued</t> that gun was inadmissable as evidence <fex name="Support">because the police didn t have a search warrant</fex>.</ex>

name: Manner
description: This FE identifes the #Manner in which an #Arguer argues.<ex>Jan <t>argued</t> <fex name="Manner">convincingly</fex> that the evidence was inadmissable. </ex>

name: Means
description: This FE identifies the #Means by which an #Arguer presents #Content to an #Addressee.<ex>Jan <t>argued</t> the evidence was admissable <fex name="Means">by waving the warrant in the air</fex>.</ex>

name: Degree
description: This FE identifies the #Degree to which an arguing event occurs.<ex>Jan <t>argued</t> the defendant s innocence <fex name="Degree">beyond a shadow of a doubt</fex>.</ex>

name: Time
description:
The Time at which an #Arguer makes an argument for the #Content.

<ex> The government <t>argued</t> <fex name="Time">in August</fex> that the prisoners should not be released</ex>

name: Place
description:
This FE identifies the location where an argument is made by an #Arguer.

<ex>The young men will be <t>arguing</t> their policies <fex name="Place">in the school auditorium</fex>.</ex>

name: Duration
description:
The period of time for which the #Arguer presents his or her reasoning to the #Addressee.

<ex>He <t>argued</t> <fex name="Duration">for hours</fex> that chess is a sport </ex>

name: Speaker
description:
The sentient entity that uses language in the written or spoken modality to reassure another person. 
<ex><fex name="Speaker">He</fex> <t>reassured</t> her that she was his favorite ice-skating star. </ex>

name: Experiencer
description:
The #Experiencer receives reassurance from the #Speaker. 
<ex>He tried to <t>reassure</t> <fex name="Exp">his potential customers</fex> that his company not only offered the best service, but also the most competitive prices. </ex>

name: Message
description:
<def-root>The <fen>Message</fen> is a proposition or set of propositions that the <fen>Speaker</fen> wants the <fen>Experiencer</fen> to believe in order to feel reassured. 

<ex>How do you <t>reassure</t> them <fex name="Msg">that you really like them</fex>?  </ex></def-root>

name: Situation
description:
Situation is the problem plaguing the #Experiencer, about which the #Speaker  reassures them.

<ex>The policeman <t>reassured</t> him <fex name="Situation">about the safety of the neighborhood</fex>. </ex>

name: Medium
description: The physical or abstract setting in which the Message is conveyed.

name: Amount_of_reassurance
description:
The amount of reassurance given.
<ex>He never really <fex name="Amount_of_reassurance">fully</fex> <t>reassured</t> me about the situation.</ex>

name: Manner
description: The #Manner in which the #Speaker reassures the <fex name="Exp">Experiencer</fex>.

name: Means
description:
The #Means by which the #Speaker reassures the #Experiencer.

<ex>Him mother reassured him <fex name="Mns">with a pat on the back</fex>.</ex>

name: Time
description: The time at which the reassurance takes place.

name: Rebel
description:
The individual or group within a society that attempts to overturn the #Current_leadership, and possibly also the #Current_order of governance.

<ex>In 1641 there was a <fex name="reb">Catholic</fex> <t>rebellion</t> against the Protestants of Ulster, and English authority over the island was shaken.</ex>

<ex>In the summer he crushed <fex name="reb">Geoffrey s</fex> <t>rebellion</t> and compelled him to renounce his claim.</ex>

name: Current_leadership
description: The people currently in power, whom the #Rebels attempt to overthrow.  <ex>It was the first time that a man of such high rank had been put to death for <t>rebellion</t> <fex name="curl">against the king</fex>.</ex>

name: Current_order
description:
The currently-existing system of authority or government.

<ex>At the time they were in <t>rebellion</t> <fex name="curo">against the Catholic government</fex>.</ex>

name: Place
description:
The location of the rebellion.

<ex>It seems probably that <t>rebellion</t> <fex name="pla">in East Anglia</fex> was as rigorously suppressed as in Kent.</ex>

name: Time
description:
The time when the rebellion takes place.

<ex>Did you support the <t>insurrection</t> <fex name="tim">of 1854</fex>?</ex>

name: Means
description:
The means by which the #Rebel rebels against the authorities.

<ex>Those actions virtually guarantee that she will be facing an <fex name="mea">armed</fex> <t>revolt</t> within the week.</ex>

name: Degree
description:
The degree to which the #Rebel opposes and fights against the #Current_leadership.

<ex>He was wary of entering into <fex name="deg">complete and utter</fex> <t>rebellion</t>.</ex>

name: Manner
description:
A description of the #Rebel or the rebellion not covered by more specific FEs.

<ex>We face an <fex name="man">increasingly violent</fex> <t>insurgency</t> with no end in sight.</ex>

name: New_order
description: The rebellion aims to install a #New_order as their leader. This FE is more frequent with noun targets.

name: Host
description: The #Host allows the Visitor to remain with him/her to acheive a goal.

name: Visitor
description: The Visitor temporarily remains with the #Host in order to achieve his/her purpose.

name: Purpose
description: The goal of the #Host in allowing the #Visitor to enter the #Host s location.

name: Recipient
description: The person who acts to come into possession of the #Theme.

name: Donor
description: The person who gives the #Theme to the #Recipient.

name: Theme
description: The entity that changes possession, beginning in the possession of the #Donor and ending up with the #Recipient.

name: Purpose_of_donor
description:
The aim of the #Donor, which they believe will be accomplished by giving  the #Theme.

<ex>Rural communities  <t>received</t> government subsidies<fex name="pur">to ensure their continued viability.</fex>.</ex>

name: Means
description:
The act that the #Recipient performs which brings about their reception of the #Theme.

<ex>She received the package <fex name="mea">by signing her name on the delivery certification</fex>.</ex>

name: Time
description:
The time at which the #Recipient comes into possession of the #Theme.

<ex>She received the package <fex name="tim">before the month was out</fex>.</ex>

name: Place
description: The location at which the Recipient comes into possession of the #Theme.

name: Mode_of_transfer
description:
The system whereby the #Theme travels (literally or metaphorically) so that the #Recipient comes into possession of the #Theme.

<ex>I <t>received</t> it <fex name="mod">by e-mail</fex>.</ex>

name: Manner
description:
The character of the #Recipient which saliently affects the action, or a general description of the action itself.

<ex>He <fex name="man">slyly</fex> <t>received</t> the package without signing for it.</ex>

name: Purpose_of_theme
description:
What the #Recipient intends to do with the #Theme.

<ex>James Madison Memorial High School <t>received</t> $1,000 <fex name="pot">to purchase instruments for school orchestra and band programs</fex>. </ex>

name: Recipient
description: The person who acts to come into possession of the #Theme.

name: Donor
description: The person who gives the #Theme to the #Recipient.

name: Theme
description: The entity that changes possession, beginning in the possession of the #Donor and ending up with the #Recipient.

name: Purpose
description:
The aim of the #Recipient, which they believe will be accomplished by accepting the #Theme.

<ex>She <t>accepted</t> the ring <fex name="pur">so that his suspicions would not be aroused</fex>.</ex>

name: Means
description:
The act that the #Recipient performs which brings about their reception of the #Theme.

<ex>She received the package <fex name="mea">by signing her name on the delivery certification</fex>.</ex>

name: Mode_of_transfer
description:
The system whereby the #Theme travels (literally or metaphorically) so that the #Recipient comes into possession of the #Theme.

<ex>I <t>received</t> it <fex name="mod">by e-mail</fex>.</ex>

name: Protagonist_1
description: The participant in a reciprocal event that is encoded as the subject of an active-form sentence or as a by-PP in a passive.

name: Protagonist_2
description: The participant in a reciprocal event that is coded in a with-PP.

name: Protagonists
description: The jointly expressed participants in a reciprocal activity.

name: Agent
description:
The #Agent is the sentient entity that creates a permanent record of the #Phenomenon.

<ex>Police informed Mr Wong that <fex name="age">they</fex> <t>recorded</t> the attack as a racial incident.</ex>

name: Phenomenon
description:
The #Phenomenon is the entity or situation in the world of which a #Agent becomes aware. 

<ex>Adams took some very good advice and meticulously <t>recorded</t> <fex name="Phen">the events that ensued</fex>.</ex>

name: Attribute
description:
That feature of the #Entity which is under discussion.


<ex>When Mohammed was sentenced to death in the Anti-Terrorism Court in Rawalpindi
the court <t>recorded</t> <fex name="Attribute">his age</fex> as  17 or 18.</ex>

name: Value
description:
The value of the #Entity that is recorded for the specified #Attribute.

<ex>In the fiscal year 2000, Nepal s imports from China and Tibet were <t>recorded</t> <fex name="Value">at
166.6 million dollars</fex>.</ex>

name: Entity
description:
An entity for which the #Agent records the #Value of an #Attribute.

<ex>At the time the Army <t>recorded</t> <fex name="ent">him</fex> as a married resident of the Town of Two Creeks.</ex>

name: Manner
description: Any description of the event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (<ment>quietly</ment>), and general descriptions comparing events (<ment>the same way</ment>). It may also indicate salient characteristics of an #Agent  that also affect the action (<ment>deliberately</ment>, <ment>eagerly</ment>, <ment>carefully</ment>).

name: Means
description: This FE identifes the action whereby the #Agent creates a record of the #Phenomenon.

name: Time
description: This FE identifies the #Time when the #Agent creates the record of the #Phenomenon.

name: Purpose
description: The goal that the #Agent has in mind in creating a record of the #Phenomenon.

name: Instrument
description: The #Agent uses an #Instrument in order to create a record of the #Phenomenon or to measure an #Entity s #Value for a specified #Attribute.

name: Medium
description:
#Medium is the physical entity in which the #Agent creates a record of their impression of the #Phenomenon.

<ex>In fact, Pepys <t>recorded</t> everything <fex name="med">in his diary</fex>.</ex>

name: Language
description:
The symbolic system used by the #Agent.

<ex> I <t>recorded</t> my notes <fex name="med">in English</fex>, usually translating from French.</ex>

name: Place
description:
The location where the #Agent sets down the facts.

<ex>From what the book says, these events were <t>recorded</t> <fex name="pla">in the Chamber of Mazarbul</fex> .</ex>

name: Record
description:
The <fex name="Record">Record</fex> is the entity in which events or accounts are permanently recorded.
<ex>She kept a <t><fex name="Record">record</fex></t> of how many times he asked to borrow money.</ex>

name: Data
description:
The #Data is the information which is recorded in the #Record.  It is often expressed as a prepositional phrase.
<ex>Andy kept a <t>log</t> <fex name="Data">of the girls he had dated</fex>.</ex>
<ex>His assistant maintained the <t>records</t> <fex name="Data">of customer payments</fex>.</ex>

name: Recorder
description:
The #Recorder is the person who records the #Data in the #Record.
<ex><fex name="Recorder">Shirley s</fex> <t>log</t> of her favorite eateries grew every day.</ex>

name: Entity
description:
The #Entity is the person or institution for which the #Record is maintained.
<ex>She keeps the <t>books</t> <fex name="Entity">for the advertising firm</fex>.</ex>
<ex><fex name="Entity">The bank s</fex> <t>records</t> were not ready for the unexpected audit.</ex>

name: Descriptor
description:
A characteristic of the #Record.

<ex>Moria s bank kept <fex name="Descriptor">detailed</fex> <t>books</t>.</ex>

name: Material
description: What a #Record is made of (if applicable).

name: Medium
description: The Medium is the language in which a Record is composed, or the physical medium (radio, ink) in which it is distributed.

name: Place
description: The region where the #Record is sold, circulated, or otherwise popular

name: Time_of_creation
description: The Time at which the #Record was created.

name: Title
description: The Title of a #Record.

name: Topic
description: Topic is what the #Record is about.

name: Use
description: The use for which the #Record was produced; its purpose.

name: Patient
description:
The    Patient may either be expressed as the External Argument or, as in Health.Cure, may occur as the possessor of the Affliction (an example of FE conflation): 
<ex><fex name="Patient">Pat</fex> is <t>convalescing</t> from surgery.</ex>
<ex>Pat s wound is <t>healing</t>.</ex>
In the second example, <ment>Pat s wound</ment> is the #Body_part, but <ment>Pat s</ment> is also an instantiation of the #Patient.

name: Affliction
description:
This FE is the injury, pain or disease experienced by the Patient.  Affliction occurs either as an External Argument of verbs or as a PP Complement of nouns or verbs: 
<ex><fex name="Affliction">The wound</fex> is <t>healing</t> well.</ex>
<ex><fex name="Affliction">Pat s wound</fex> is <t>healing</t> well.</ex>
<ex>Pat is <t>recovering</t> <fex name="Affliction">from scarlet fever</fex>.</ex>
<ex>Pat s <t>recovery</t> <fex name="Affliction">from scarlet fever</fex> was very rapid. </ex>

name: Body_part
description:
Where Body Part is expressed as a separate constituent (rather than in phrases such as <ment>the wound on Pat s arm</ment>), it occurs as an External Argument: 
<ex><fex name="Body_part">Pat s arm</fex> <t>healed</t> up quickly.</ex>
 Body Part is treated as a separate Frame Element although it may be viewed as metonymic for Affliction.

name: Manner
description: Manner of performing an action

name: Degree
description: The extent to which the #Patient s state diverges from their former illness.

name: Means
description:
An act of the #Patient that results in their recovery.
<ex>You will <t>recover</t> more quickly <fex name="Mns">by getting simple bed-rest</fex>.</ex>

name: Time
description: The time at which the recovery takes place.

name: Place
description: The location where the recovery takes place.

name: Theme
description: The entity that is in motion, and changes path due to the action of an Agent or due to a Cause.

name: Original_path
description:
The route that the #Theme was originally headed for.  In cases when the #Theme is an intentional actor or is controlled by an intentional actor, the #Original_path correlates with the (former) plan of the actor.  In other cases, the #Original_path corresponds to what would be expected for the path of the #Theme from general principles or physics.  (In some cases, the #Original_path may indicate the intended destination, and not just the path, of the #Theme.)

<ex>VTA <t>detoured</t> motorists <fex name="ori">from southbound I-880</fex> onto Airport Boulevard to access Coleman Avenue.</ex>

name: Path
description:
The #Path refers to (a part of) the ground over which the #Theme ends up travelling after being diverted.


<ex>Traffic was <t>redirected</t> Tuesday <fex name="Path">along Melrose Avenue</fex> to make way for the scoreboard columns, Mellecker said.</ex>

name: Goal
description:
The location the #Theme ends up in after being redirected.


<ex>Not knowing any better, he <t>redirected</t> people <fex name="Goal">to the city center</fex>.</ex>

name: Direction
description:
This FE is used for expressions that indicate motion along a line from the current deitic center towards a reference point (which may be implicit) that does not necessarily correspond to the #Goal.  Often #Direction is defined with reference to the canonical orientation of the #Theme, the orientation imposed by an implicit observer, or the inherent orientation of the scene.

<ex>Flight 93 left Newark, NJ, earlier that day, bound for San Francisco, but it was hijacked by four terrorists who <t>diverted</t> it <fex name="dir">back toward Washington, DC</fex> . </ex>

name: Agent
description:
An individual that determines the change in course of the #Theme.

<ex>When we ended up over in Marrakech, or Dakar, <fex name="age">they</fex> <t>diverted</t> us up to Tobruk.</ex>

name: Cause
description:
An event that brings about the change in the #Path of the #Theme.

<ex>We even got a bonus morning in Tokyo s international airport when <fex name="Cause">a bad storm</fex>
<t>diverted</t> us on the way to Asia.</ex>

name: Time
description:
This FE identifies the #Time when the redirection of motion occurs.

<ex><fex name="Time">Last Tuesday</fex> they were <t>detouring</t> everybody down onto 5th avenue .</ex>

name: Manner
description:
Any description of the redirection which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (<ment>quietly, loudly</ment>), and general descriptions comparing events (<ment>the same way</ment>). In addition, it may indicate salient characteristics of the #Agent that affect the action (<ment>presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully</ment>). 

<ex>Anything flagged as suspect by this system would be <fex name="Manr">carefully</fex> <t>diverted</t> to another nearby area, remote from public access. and well shielded, for a second screening .</ex>

name: Place
description:
The #Place is the location at which the redirection occurs.

<ex>They <t>redirected</t> us <fex name="">at the corner of Alstone and Bokchoy Avenue</fex>.</ex>

name: Purpose
description:
The state-of-affairs that the Agent intends to bring about by redirecting the #Theme.

<ex>She was talking about how the passengers <t>diverted</t> the plane in order <fex name="pur">to protect
the nation s capital</fex>.</ex>

name: Means
description: The method by which the #Agent redirects the #Theme.

name: Role
description: Role of each member of the refereeing at the soccer game.

name: Specification
description: Specification of the function performed by a member of the refereeing.

name: Origin
description: The place from where the person is.

name: Referee
description: The person in charge of ensuring that the rules of the game will be followed.

name: Action
description: The referee may take several actions during the match, such as controlling its duration, awarding goals, conceding compensations and applying sanctions to the players.

name: Source_of_information
description:
The #Source_of_information is referred to as a source of information related to the current text.

<ex>The accreting matter forms a disc whose instabilities trigger outbursts (<t>see</t> <fex name="soi">Lasota 2001</fex> for a review of the instability model).</ex>

name: Information
description:
The #Source_of_information contains #Information that the reader is directed to.

<ex>The accreting matter forms a disc whose instabilities trigger outbursts (<t>see</t> Lasota 2001 <fex name="inf">for a review of the instability model</fex>). </ex>

name: Reference_universe
description: Reference_universe

name: Constrast_universe
description: Universo_de_contraste

name: Speaker
description: The person who uses the #Name to refer to the #Entity.

name: Entity
description: The entity that is designated by the #Speaker with the #Name used.

name: Name
description: The linguistic form used by the #Speaker to refer to the #Entity.

name: Time
description: The point of time or interval when the #Speaker uses the #Name.

name: Agent
description: The sentient entity that effects a change in the #Entity.

name: Entity
description:
This FE denotes a complex, abstract, human-created entity with interdependent parts which has either deteriorated or is no longer adequate for its purpose given a change in circumstances and which the #Agent therefore wants to modify.

<ex>Particular attention will be devoted to recent proposals to <t>reform</t> <fex name="ent">UK competition policy</fex>.</ex>

name: Cause
description:
This FE is for non-human causes, such as a processes or institutions, responsible for reform.  

<ex>Thus , <fex name="Cause">radical reform</fex> <t>restructures</t> rape offences in ways that it is hoped will facilitate the prosecution process without jeopardising the interests of the accused .</ex>

name: Time
description:
The time at which the change of the #Entity is caused.

<ex>The House of Representatives, on a bipartisan basis, sought to <t>reform</t> the program <fex name="Time">this year</fex>.</ex>

name: Degree
description:
The extent to which the #Entity differs from its former state at the end of the reform.

<ex>I am trying to <fex name="deg">completely</fex> <t>overhaul</t> my diet, and i need help.</ex>

name: Manner
description: Any description of the  event causing the change of the #Entity which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (<ment>quietly</ment>), general descriptions comparing events (<ment>the same way</ment>), and salient characteristics of the #Agent that also affect the action (<ment>carefully</ment>).

name: Purpose
description:
An action that the #Agent intends to accomplish by effecting a change in the #Entity.

<ex>Indeed, as the world becomes increasingly paperless, it would be prudent to consider <t>overhauling</t> the rules <fex name="pur">in order to adapt them to a digitalized society</fex>.</ex>

name: Initial_category
description:
The category of which the #Entity is a member before the #Agent causes a change in its makeup.

<ex>Since 1986, the Vietnamese Government has performed a  doi moi  (renovation)
policy that <t>reformed</t> the economy <fex name="ini">from central planning economy</fex> to market.</ex>

name: Final_category
description:
The category that the #Entity belongs to after it has undergone the change.

<ex>We have also <t>reformed</t> the office managing system from a "proprietorship" <fex name="Final_category">to a "partnership"</fex>.</ex>

name: Means
description: This FE identifies the steps or actions directly enabling reform.

name: Duration
description: The length of time over which reforming takes place

name: Place
description: The place where the reformation occurs.

name: Cognizer
description:
The #Cognizer is the one that makes a #Judgment. This role is typically expressed as the External Argument (or in a <ment>by</ment>-PP in passives).

<ex><fex name="Cognizer">The boss</fex> <t>appreciates</t> you for your diligence.</ex>
<ex><fex name="Cognizer">The boss</fex> is very <t>appreciative</t> of my work.</ex>

name: Evaluee
description:
#Evaluee is the person or thing that a judgment is made with respect to. With verbs this FE is typically expressed as Object: 
<ex>He <t>regarded</t> <fex name="eva">her</fex> poorly.</ex>

name: Judgment
description:
A description (from the point of view of the #Cognizer) of the position of the #Evaluee on a scale of approval.

<ex>I hold him in the <fex name="jud">highest</fex> <t>esteem</t>.</ex>

name: Manner
description: Any description of the #Cognizer s judgment which is not covered by more specific FEs, including general descriptions of events (<ment>the same way, presumptuously, secretly</ment>).

name: Extent_of_acclaim
description: The #Extent_of_acclaim is a description of the number or locales of the #Cognizers.  <ex>He is <fex name="ext">widely</fex> held in high <t>regard</t>.</ex>

name: Interior
description: A region of space located inside a #Ground.

name: Ground
description: An entity whose location is relatively well-known and which, thus, can serve as a land-mark to identify the #Interior, #Surface, or #Boundary.

name: Surface
description: The region located outside of, but in contact with, the #Ground.

name: Boundary
description: The region associated with the boundary of the #Ground.

name: Exterior
description: The region which is not located inside or in contact with the #Ground.

name: Agent
description: The #Agent (most commonly a group of people) is responsible for an attempted rejection of leadership.

name: Old_leader
description: With words denoting removal (or attempted removal) from office of a leader, the #Old_leader is typically the direct object of verbs, or occurs in a PP Complement with noun targets, frequently headed by the preposition against.

name: New_leader
description: With words denoting appointment to a position, the #New_leader is typically the object of verbs.

name: Role
description: Frequently the #Old_leader or #New_leader is referred to only by his/her role. However, the #Role can be expressed separately from the leader.

name: Old_order
description: It identifies the political order that existed before the change of leadership occurs.

name: Function
description: The #Function is the purpose the #New_leader will fulfill.

name: Body
description: A group of individuals who are elected or appointed to perform some function of governance.

name: Degree
description: It indicates the #Degree to which an event occurs.

name: Manner
description: It is used for the #Manner of performing an action.

name: Means
description: It identifies the #Means by which the #Agent attempts to accomplish the change of leadership.

name: Time
description: When the change takes place.

name: Place
description: Where the change takes place.

name: Purpose
description: The situation that the #Agent attempts to bring about by the change of leadership.

name: Entity
description:
The concrete or abstract thing that is brought into an earlier state in which it was more effective and powerful.

<ex>The <t>reinvigoration</t> <fex name="ent">of the tourism</fex> economy is recognised by many as an ambitious but necessary objective.</ex>

name: Agent
description:
The #Agent puts the #Entity into a state of renewed vigor.

<ex><fex name="Agt">We</fex> <t>revitalized</t> the Golden Mountaineer Card, and we gave seniors a prescription drug benefit.</ex>

name: Cause
description:
An event or state whose occurrence brings about the #Entity s return to a vigorous state.

<ex>Rock has been <t>rejuvenated</t> creatively and commercially <fex name="cau">by hip-hop-inspired rock acts</fex>.</ex>

name: Place
description:
This FE identifies the place where the Agent brings about the change in the #Entity s state.


<ex>New York s distinction as America s tax cut leader has <t>revitalized</t> the economy
<fex name="Place">in every corner of the state</fex>.</ex>

name: Purpose
description:
This FE identifies the purpose for which an #Agent brings about the revitalization of the #Entity.

<ex>Older neighborhoods throughout the area should be <t>revitalized</t> <fex name="Purp">in order to be retained as neighborhoods having unique identities</fex>.</ex>

name: Time
description:
This FE identifies the time when the #Agent causes the renewal of the #Entity.

<ex>Production was <t>revitalized</t> <fex name="Time">last June</fex>.</ex>

name: Means
description:
This FE identifies the action that the #Agent performs in order so as to bring about the reinvigoration of the #Entity.

<ex>Natalie Cole <t>resuscitated</t> her career <fex name="Means">by recording a posthumous duet with her father on his old hit "Unforgettable"</fex>.</ex>

name: Manner
description:
Any description of the intentional act which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (<ment>quietly, loudly</ment>), and general descriptions comparing events (<ment>the same way</ment>). In addition, it may indicate salient characteristics of an #Agent that also affect the action (<ment>presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully</ment>). 

<ex>They assessed the effectiveness of four different species of native trees for <fex name="Mnr">rapid</fex> <t>rejuvenation</t> of the mined land.</ex>

name: Instrument
description: The device used by the #Agent to help accomplish the action indicated by the target.

name: Concept_1
description:
The concept which is linked to #Concept_2.  

<ex>High death rates among smokers <t>connect</t> <fex name="Concept_1">cigarettes</fex> with disease.</ex>

name: Concept_2
description:
The concept which is tied to #Concept_1.  

<ex>Skid marks on the street <t>connects</t> my neighbor s car <fex name="Concept_2">to my broken mailbox</fex>.</ex>

name: Evidence
description:
The phenomenon that points to or proves the connection between #Concept_1 and #Concept_2. Generally expressed as an external.

<ex><fex name="Evidence">Footprints</fex> <t>connect</t> her to the crime.</ex>

name: Concepts
description:
The two concepts expressed jointly.

<ex><fex name="Concept_1">Ample evidence</fex> is able to <t>connect</t> <fex name="Concepts">the two</fex>.</ex>

name: Point_of_view
description:
The point of view from which the connection exists.

<ex><fex name="Point_of_view">As far as we are concerned</fex> this evidence <t>ties</t> the secretary to the case of the missing apple fritters.</ex>

name: Degree
description:
The #Degree of connection between the #Concepts.

<ex>This <fex name="Degree">definitively</fex> <t>connects</t> our water shortage problems to the ascendance of a new governor.</ex>

name: Specification
description:
A further specification on the linkage.

<ex>The evidence <fex name="Specification">causally</fex> <t>links</t> these two.</ex>

name: Entity_1
description: An entity with a relationship to #Entity_2.

name: Entity_2
description: The entity with a relationship to #Entity_1.

name: Entities
description: The things that are related to each other. Collective expression of the FEs #Entity_1 and #Entity_2.

name: Relation_type
description: A characterization of the relation that determines what model is active for further reasoning.

name: Profiled_region
description: The region which is located relative to the Ground and which is shaped according to an image schema.

name: Ground
description: An entity whose location is relatively well-known and which, thus, can serve as a land-mark to identify the #Profiled_region.

name: Relative_location
description: A place that a #Profiled_region is located with respect to.

name: Focal_feature
description: A stable bounded area defined by its relation to another landform. This FE is incorporated in each LU in this frame.

name: Landmark_feature
description: The landform with respect to which the #Focal_feature is defined.

name: Constituent_parts
description: Salient parts of the #Focal_feature.

name: Relative_location
description: A place that a #Focal_feature is located with respect to.

name: Type
description: The #Type is the subtype of natural feature.

name: Container_possessor
description:
The political locale that the #Locale is a the head of.
<ex>People come from all over the nation to work in and visit the <t>capital</t> <fex name="ContP">of the United States</fex>.</ex>

name: Locale
description:
The politically or administratively-defined locale that functions as the head of state for the containing entity.

<ex>Most of the major government buildings are in the center of the <fex name="Lcl"><t>capital</t></fex></ex>

name: Constituent_parts
description: The materials or #Constituent_parts that make up a #Locale.

name: Name
description:
This FE is used for the Names of Locales.


<ex>Gyeonggi Province is located in the west-central area of the Korean Peninsula, and surrounds the Korean <t>capital</t> <fex name="Name">of Seoul</fex>.</ex>

name: Relative_location
description:
A place that the #Locale is located with respect to.

<ex>All the <t>capitals</t> <fex name="RelLoc">in Canada</fex> are clean.</ex>

name: Descriptor
description:
Any description, characteristic, or property of the #Locale which is not covered by more specific FEs.

<ex>He essentially was the head of the <fex name="des">largest</fex> <t>county seat</t> <fex name="des">in northern California</fex>.</ex>

name: Population
description: The people living in the jurisdiction of the locale, typically expressed with a PP-<ment>of</ment>.

name: Rate
description: The number which represents the relative quantities of the #Numerator and the #Denominator.

name: Numerator
description: Identifies the quantity whose magnitude is determined relative to the #Denominator.

name: Focal_entity
description: The set of things to which the #Rate pertains.

name: Denominator
description: The quantity whose magnitude is determined relative to the #Numerator.

name: Descriptor
description: Any characteristic of the #Rate that is not covered by another more specific FE.

name: Type
description: A name of the #Rate of or description of the particular category the #Rate belongs to.

name: Individual_1
description: The individual being described in terms of #Individual_2.

name: Individual_2
description: The individual whose identity serves to identify #Individual_2.

name: Individuals
description: The individuals in a (symmetrically construed) #Relation to each other.

name: Relation
description: The type of connection between the #Individuals.

name: Focal_occasion
description: The #Focal_occasion is the event which is  being located in time with respect to some other event: The #Focal_occasion is profiled, in contrast to the #Landmark_occasion, which is backgrounded.

name: Landmark_occasion
description: The #Landmark_occasion is the event  with respect to which the #Focal_occasion is located.

name: Focal_participant
description: The #Focal_participant is the participant in the #Focal_occasion which is profiled as being in a relation of relative time to the #Landmark_occasion.

name: Interval
description: Frequently, it is the Interval between the #Landmark_occasion and the #Focal_occasion.

name: Degree
description: The #Degree to which event occurs.

name: Manner
description: The #Manner of performing an action.

name: Captor
description:
The #Captor is the individual who ends the confinement or captivity of the #Theme. The release of the #Theme is in accord with the plans of the #Captor.

<ex><fex name="cap">The police</fex> <t>released</t> the suspect.</ex>

name: Theme
description:
The #Theme is the person or object in motion released by the #Captor.

<ex>The police <t>released</t> <fex name="Theme">the suspect</fex>.</ex>

name: Location_of_confinement
description:
The location to which the #Theme s motion was formerly inhibited.

<ex>She petitioned unsuccessfully for their <t>release</t> <fex name="Loc">from Chateau D If</fex>.</ex>

name: Manner
description:
Any description of the release which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (<ment>quietly, loudly</ment>), and general descriptions comparing events (<ment>the same way</ment>). In addition, it may indicate salient characteristics of an #Captor that also affect the action (<ment>presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully</ment>). 

<ex>The police <t>released</t> the suspect <fex name="Manner">carefully</fex>.</ex>

name: Means
description:
The action of the #Captor which brings about the release.

<ex>He <t>released</t> a hundred prisoners <fex name="Means">by signing a single document.</fex>.</ex>

name: Purpose
description: The #Purpose identifies the purpose for which the #Captor release the #Theme.

name: Place
description:
The location where the #Theme ceases to be confined.

<ex>"Aren t you going to <t>release</t> us <fex name="Place">here</fex>?" she asked.</ex>

name: Time
description: The #Time identifies when the #Captor release the #Theme .

name: Authorities
description:
The #Authorities are those who have the official authority to release a #Suspect.

<ex><fex name="Auth">The police</fex> <t>released</t> the suspect.</ex>

name: Suspect
description:
The #Suspect is the person released by the #Authorities, having been held under suspicion of having committed a crime.

<ex>The police <t>released</t> <fex name="Susp">the suspect</fex>.</ex>

name: Holding_location
description: The place that the #Suspect is no longer confined to.

name: Manner
description:
The #Manner is the way in which the #Authorities release the #Suspect.

<ex>The police <t>released</t> the suspect <fex name="Manner">carefully</fex>.</ex>

name: Means
description: The #Means identifies the means by which the #Authorities release the #Suspect.

name: Purpose
description: The #Purpose identifies the purpose for which the #Authorities release the #Suspect.

name: Place
description: The #Place identifies the place where the #Authorities release the #Suspect .

name: Time
description: The #Time identifies when the #Authorities release the #Suspect .

name: Protagonist
description:
The person or thing whose needs are served by the #Means action.

<ex> <fex name="Protagonist">I</fex> <t>rely</t> on this walking stick to get me around</ex>

name: Benefit
description:
An action or state that the #Means action brings about and from which the #Protagonist profits.

<ex>He still <t>relies</t> on me <fex name="Benefit">to do the head work</fex></ex>

name: Intermediary
description:
The person or thing that performs a #Means action to the #Benefit of the #Protagonist.

<ex>We <t>rely</t>  <fex name="Intermediary">on you</fex> for feedback and input</ex>

name: Instrument
description:
An object that the #Protagonist uses to realize a #Benefit.

<ex>I hate having to <t>rely</t> <fex name="Instrument">on my car</fex> to get where I want to go</ex>

name: Purpose
description:
An action or state that the #Protagonist wants to bring about.  It is more or less the #Benefit viewed in relation to the #Protagonist rather than the #Means.

<ex>The user will quickly begin to <t>rely</t> on the pills <fex name="Purpose">to cope with life pressures</fex>.</ex>

name: Means
description:
An action performed either by the #Protagonist or an #Intermediary that has a #Benefit for  the #Protagonist attached to it.


<ex>We <t>rely</t> <fex name="Means">on your response</fex></ex>

name: Degree
description:
The extent to which the achievement of the #Benefit depends on the #Means action.

<ex> He knows I <t>rely</t> <fex name="Degree">entirely</fex> on him to point out all my blunders and spelling mistakes.</ex>

name: Cognizer
description: An individual that considers the Expectation certain.

name: Expectation
description:
The fact which the #Cognizer depends on in planning for the future.

<ex>I think you should <t>count</t> <fex name="Expectation">on getting a paycut</fex>.</ex>

name: Believer
description: The #Believer is the person whose religious beliefs are in question.

name: Content
description: The #Content is the object of the #Believer's religious conviction.

name: Role
description: It is a  function that an #Element of the faith is believed to serve.

name: Element
description: A predefined, fixed idea to which reference is made throughout the belief system.

name: Degree
description: It identifies the #Degree to which the #Believer is convinced of the usefulness and validity of their religious beliefs.

name: Manner
description: It identifies the #Manner in which the #Believer knows or thinks something.

name: Remainder
description: The part of the Resource that the Process has not yet applied to.

name: Resource
description: The original set of items to which the Depletive_process applies one after the other.

name: Original_owner
description:
The individual who owned the Resource that is being depleted.

<ex>Only two poems are <t>left</t> <fex name="oo">from Virgil Smithers</fex></ex>

name: Original_context
description: A temporal setting or event which metonymically stands for the entities that were in existence then. The #Remainder is part of the #Original_context. This FE occurs especially when abstract entities such as social institutions are seen as #Resources.  <ex>Only the Beaux Arts cinema is <t>left</t> <fex name="oc">from the golden era of the 1940s</fex></ex>

name: Location
description:
The place where the Resource is found.

<ex>How much is <t>left</t> <fex name="loc">in the treasury</fex>?</ex>

name: Time
description:
The Time when the amount of the Resource that is let is assessed.

<ex><fex name="tm">In 1910</fex>, only two of the Renaissance buildings <t>remained</t></ex>

name: Cognizer
description:
A person who recalls memories of past experience or impressions.


<ex>I will truly miss Delmar and <fex name="cog">I</fex> will always <t>remember</t> when his eyes lit up everytime he got ice-cream.</ex>

name: Salient_entity
description:
A person or thing that the #Cognizer remembers as being involved in some state or event or as having some property.

<ex>today I saw a sister s exclassmate, and didn t recognise him, I <t>remember</t> <fex name="sal">him</fex> taller.</ex>

name: Experience
description:
A past event or state that the #Cognizer witnessed and is now recalling. The #Experience may be a specific event or state or a vaguer period that is treated as a unit.

<ex> I can still <t>remember</t> <fex name="exp">saying the pledge every morning before class in elementary school</fex>, a rare occurrence among elementary school kids nowadays. </ex>

name: Impression
description:
A property that the #Cognizer attributes to the #Salient_entity based on their past experience of the #Salient_entity or a category to which the #Salient_entity is assigned.

<ex>I <t>remember</t> Peter <fex name="imp">as immensely kind in those first three weeks before I was packed off to boarding school</fex>.</ex>

name: State
description:
A property or role that a #Salient_entity actually held and which frames the memories that the #Cognizer has of the #Salient_entity.

<ex>Atulprasad is principally <t>remembered</t> <fex name="sta">as a musician and composer</fex>. </ex>

name: Vividness
description:
The degree of detail and/or immediacy of  a remembered #Experience.

<ex>Brian Wooley only <fex name="viv">dimly</fex> <t>remembered</t> the hand falling on his shoulder</ex>

name: Context
description:
A situation in which the Cognizer encountered the #Salient_entity.

<ex>She was just how I <t>remember</t> her <fex name="con">from school</fex> .</ex>

name: Time
description:
The time when the #Cognizer remembers the experience.

<ex>I  ll <fex name="tim">never</fex> <t>forget</t> being in hospital</ex>

name: Manner
description:
Any description of the event (or state) which is not covered by more specific FEs, including epistemic modification (probably, presumably, mysteriously) and general descriptions comparing events (the same way). It may also indicate salient characteristics of the #Cognizer that affect the action (fondly). 

<ex>Bill will be <fex name="man">fondly</fex> <t>remembered</t> by all.</ex>

name: Duration
description:
The amount of time for which the #Cognizer remembers the experience.

<ex> Your generosity and kindness will be long <t>remembered</t> <fex name="dur">for years to come</fex>. </ex>

name: Cognizer
description:
The person who retains and retrieves information.

<ex>I bet <fex name="cog">Johno</fex> can <t>remember</t> the name of the Ramones  drummer.</ex>

name: Mental_content
description:
#Mental_content stored by and accessible to the #Cognizer. It may derive from experience or activity or simply from being informed of it.

<ex>Do you <t>remember</t> <fex name="men">how to spell cupboard ?</fex></ex>

name: Accuracy
description:
The extent to which the remembered #Mental_content matches the information or experience that it is based on.

<ex>If I <t>remember</t> <fex name="acc">correctly</fex> the pre-War price for hanging a wall up, a roll of wallpaper was about sixpence a roll , and that included preparing as well.</ex>

name: Time
description:
The time when the #Cognizer retrieves #Mental_content or forgets it.

<ex> <fex name="tim">Afterwards</fex>, I <t>forgot</t> what I was pissed off about, but I was. </ex>

name: Topic
description:
An entity, concrete or abstract, or an event that the remembered #Mental_content is about.


<ex>The boy who suffered brain damage when he was hit by a vehicle while walking into school testified Friday he <t>remembers</t> nothing <fex name="top">about being hit</fex>. </ex>

name: Context
description:
A situation or a text from which the #Mental_content was acquired.

<ex>You will <t>remember</t> <fex name="con">from grade school</fex> that you do not want to make the nuns impatient.</ex>

name: Cognizer
description:
The person who thinks of or fails to think of an obligatory or desireable #Action.

<ex>.<fex name="cog">Alex and Zach Swindle</fex>, Centre, visited their brother in San Francisco in June and <t>remembered</t> to take along a copy of The Post>. </ex>

name: Action
description:
An #Action that the #Cognizer is supposed to carry out. This may be a Task imposed specifically on the #Cognizer or it may be self- or socially imposed.

<ex>This time we <t>remembered</t> <fex name="act">to turn the gas off before leaving</fex>.</ex>

name: Salient_entity
description:
The #Salient_entity is a person or thing that the #Cognizer needs to affect or effect when carrying out the #Action.

<ex>We piled into the taxi and right when we were about to leave, I realized we <t>forgot</t> <fex name="sal">the video camera</fex></ex>

name: Time
description:
A #Time when the #Cognizer remembers or fails to carry out an #Action.


<ex>He <t>remembered</t> <fex name="tim">afterwards</fex> to kiss her fingers.</ex>

name: Frequency
description: This FE describes how often the Action is performed by the Cognizer.

name: Agent
description:
The #Agent is the person (or other force) that causes the #Theme to move. 

<ex><fex name="Agent">The waiter</fex> <t>removed</t> the dishes from the table.</ex>

name: Theme
description:
Theme is the object that changes location. 

<ex>The waiter <t>removed</t> <fex name="Theme">the dishes</fex> from the table. </ex>

name: Source
description:
The #Source is the initial location of the #Theme, before it changes location.

<ex>The waiter <t>removed</t> the dishes <fex name="Source">from the table</fex>.</ex>

A few of the words in this frame participate in an alternation between a #Theme object and a #Source object.

<ex>The waiter <t>cleared</t> <fex name="Thm">the dishes</fex> <fex name="Src">from the table</fex>.</ex>
<ex>The waiter <t>cleared</t> <fex name="Src">the table</fex> <fex name="Thm">of dishes</fex>.</ex>
<ex>The plumber <t>drained</t> <fex name="Thm">the water</fex> <fex name="Src">from the sink</fex>.</ex>
<ex>The plumber <t>drained</t> <fex name="Src"> the sink</fex> <fex name="Thm">of water</fex>.</ex>
<ex>The gardener <t>emptied</t> <fex name="Thm">the soil</fex> <fex name="Src">from the bucket</fex>.</ex>
<ex>The gardener <t>emptied</t> <fex name="Src">the bucket</fex> <fex name="Thm">of soil</fex>.</ex>
<ex>The army <t>evacuated</t> <fex name="Thm">the people</fex> <fex name="Src">from the tow

name: Cause
description:
The non-Agentive cause of the removing event.

<ex><fex name="Cause">The raging fire</fex> quickly <t>emptied</t> the square of all people.</ex>
<ex><fex name="Cause">Magnetic storms on the sun</fex> sometimes <t>eject</t> hot gas from beneath the surface.</ex>

name: Path
description:
Any description of a trajectory of motion which is neither a #Source nor a #Goal expresses the frame element #Path. In this frame, #Path expressions almost always have a <ment>via</ment>-sense. 

<ex>The army <t>evacuated</t> the townspeople <fex name="Path">through the tunnel</fex>. </ex>

name: Goal
description:
The #Goal is the location where the #Theme ends up. This Frame Element is not profiled by words in this frame, though it may occasionally be expressed, as with the word <ment>remove</ment>.

<ex>Grandmother <t>removed</t> the fine china <fex name="Goal">to a safe place</fex>.</ex>

name: Manner
description:
Any expression which describes a property of motion which is not directly related to the trajectory of motion expresses the frame element #Manner. Descriptions of speed, steadiness, grace, means of motion, and other things count as Manner expressions.

<ex>The army <t>evacuated</t> the townspeople <fex name="Manner">efficiently</fex></ex>

name: Means
description:
The #Means is an act whereby the #Agent achieves the removal.

<ex>You ll have to <t>eject</t> it <fex name="Mns">by sticking an unfolded paperclip into that little hole</fex>.</ex>

name: Degree
description: Degree to which event occurs

name: Distance
description:
The #Distance is any expression which characterizes the extent of motion.  This frame element occurs throughout the motion domain but is very infrequent in Motion_Noise. 

<ex>John <t>withdrew</t> the gun <fex name="Distance">a few feet</fex>.</ex>

name: Time
description:
The time at which the removal takes place.

<ex><fex name="Time">That year</fex> they drained the swamp-water out of Higgs  bottomland.</ex>

name: Place
description:
The location where the removal takes place.

<ex>You just take the dishes and <t>rinse</t> them <fex name="pla">in the kitchen</fex>, okay?</ex>

name: Instrument
description: The tool the #Agent uses to remove the #Theme from its #Source.

name: Purpose
description: To what end the #Agent or #Cause removes the #Theme.

name: Direction
description: This FE is used to indicate the #Direction that the #Theme travels when it is removed.

name: Agent
description:
The #Agent is the entity that does the emptying. 
<ex><fex name="Agent">Pat</fex> <t>cleared</t> the table of dishes.</ex>

name: Theme
description:
The #Theme, when overtly expressed, invariably occurs in a PP Complement headed by "of".
<ex>Pat <t>cleared</t> the table <fex name="Thm">of dishes</fex>.</ex>

name: Source
description: The #Source is the region or container which is emptied of something.  In this frame, the #Source occurs as an NP Object. <ex>Pat <t>cleared</t> <fex name="Src">the table</fex>.</ex>

name: Cause
description: The #Cause is the non-sentient force which instigates the emptying. <ex><fex name="Cause">A basic washing</fex> can <t>degrease</t> <fex name="Source">your workclothes</fex>.</ex>

name: Path
description:
The #Path is any description of a trajectory of motion which is neither a #Source nor a #Goal.
<ex>Kim <t>emptied</t> the basin <fex name="Path">over the edge of the balcony</fex>.</ex>

name: Goal
description:
The #Goal is any expression which tells where the #Theme ends up, or would end up, as a result of the motion.
<ex>The children <t>emptied</t> the toy box <fex name="Goal">onto the floor</fex>.</ex>

name: Degree
description: The #Degree to which the #Agent empties the #Source.

name: Manner
description: The #Manner in which the #Agent empties the #Source.

name: Means
description: The #Means by which the #Agent empties the #Source.

name: Instrument
description:
This is the object with which the #Agent empties the #Source.
<ex>I <t>emptied</t> the tub <fex name="Inst">with a bucket</fex>.</ex>
<ex>I <t>peeled</t> the oranges <fex name="Inst">with a knife</fex>.</ex>

name: Place
description:
The location where the emptying takes place.

<ex>Don t <t>eviscerate</t> him <fex name="pla">in the house</fex>!</ex>

name: Time
description:
The time at which the emptying takes place.

<ex>The prisoners were <t>deloused</t> <fex name="tim">in June and August</fex>.</ex>

name: Agent
description: The sentient entity causes the Artifact to not function.

name: Artifact
description: The device that no longer works.

name: Cause
description: An inanimate entity or force that renders the #Artifact non-functional.

name: Means
description:
The action that the #Agent performs which results in the #Artifact being inoperable.

<ex>I guess I <t>broke</t> the doorknob <fex name="Mns">by twisting it too hard</fex>.</ex>

name: Time
description: The time at which the event takes place.

name: Place
description: The place at which the event takes place.

name: Manner
description: A holistic description of the event (e.g. <ment>the same way</ment>), including depictions of the #Agent in terms of how the state of the #Agent affects the overall action (e.g <ment>maliciously</ment>).

name: Depictive
description: This FE is used for any #Depictive phrase describing the state of particpant while lack of functionality is being rendered.

name: Lessee
description:
The individual that gains temporary use of the #Goods by paying the #Lessor

<ex><fex name="Lessee">We</fex>  ve been <t>renting</t> from him for years .</ex>

name: Goods
description:
The FE #Goods is an item which is temporarily exchanged for #Money in a transaction. 
<ex>I tried to <t>hire</t> <fex name="Goods">a bicycle</fex> .</ex>

name: Means
description:
The means by which a commercial transaction occurs.
<ex> Will they allow you to <t>lease</t> the airfield <fex name="Mns">by check</fex>? </ex>

name: Money
description:
#Money is the thing given in exchange for tempory use of the #Goods.
<ex>Sam <t>chartered</t> the ship <fex name="Money">for only $12,000</fex>.</ex>

name: Rate
description:
In isome cases, price or payment is described per unit of #Goods.
<ex> Jon <t>rented</t> a car for <fex name="Rate">twenty dollars a day</fex> . </ex>

name: Lessor
description:
The individual who has permanent ownership of the #Goods and allows them to be used by the Lessee temporarily.

<ex>Kim and Pat <t>rent</t> the apartment <fex name="Lessor">from Jess</fex>. </ex>

name: Unit
description:
This FE is any unit in which goods or services can be measured.
Generally, it occurs in a <ment>by</ment>-PP. 
<ex>Lee <t>rents</t> rooms <fex name="Unit">by the hour</fex>. </ex>

name: Place
description:
The location the renting takes place.

<ex>I <t>rented</t> the car <fex name="Place">right next to the airport</fex> .</ex>

name: Purpose
description:
The purpose for which the renting is performed.

<ex>Though he was already wanted , he <t>rented</t> a car <fex name="Purpose">to make a getaway</fex> .</ex>

name: Time
description: When the event occurs.

name: Manner
description: Any description of the purchasing event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (<ment>quietly, loudly</ment>), and general descriptions comparing events (<ment>the same way</ment>). It may also indicate salient characteristics of the #Lessee that affect the action (<ment>presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully</ment>). <ex>She quit her old job and <fex name="Manner">gleefully</fex> <t>leased</t> a cheap, Upper East Side apartment.</ex>

name: Duration
description:
The length of time that the #Goods are (or have been) in the #Lessee s possession.  

<ex>Our neighbors have been <t>renting</t> their house <fex name="Duration">for the last ten years</fex>.</ex>

name: Lessee
description:
The #Lessee has #Money and wants the #Goods.
<ex>Lee <t>rented</t> a textbook <fex name="Lessee">to Abby</fex>.</ex>

name: Goods
description:
The FE #Goods is the item which is temporarily exchanged for #Money. 
<ex>Kim <t>rented</t> <fex name="Goods">the shed</fex> out .</ex>

name: Lessor
description:
The #Lessor has possession of the #Goods and temporarily exchanges them for #Money from a #Lessee.
<ex>So far, <fex name="Lessor">my company</fex> has <t>rented</t> the video more than three million times .</ex>

name: Means
description:
The means by which a commercial transaction occurs.
<ex>Abby <t>rented</t> her room <fex name="Means">by putting an ad in the Examiner</fex>.</ex>

name: Money
description:
Money is the thing given in exchange for Goods in a transaction.
<ex>The x-ray machine was <t>leased</t> <fex name="Money">for $12,000</fex> to a small firm in Menlo Park .</ex>

name: Rate
description:
In some cases, price or payment is described per unit of #Goods.
<ex> The hostel <t>rents</t> a bunk <fex name="Rate">for about 20 dollars a night</fex> .</ex>

name: Unit
description:
This FE is any unit in which goods or services can be measured.
Generally, it occurs in a <ment>by</ment>-PP. 
<ex>Bob <t>rents</t> rooms <fex name="Unit">by the hour</fex>. </ex>

name: Place
description:
The location where the leasing takes place.

<ex>U-haul <t>rents</t> <fex name="Place">almost everywhere</fex>.</ex>

name: Purpose
description: The purpose for which an intentional act is performed.

name: Time
description: The time at which the renting takes place.

name: Manner
description:
Any description of the renting event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (<ment>quietly, loudly</ment>), and general descriptions comparing events (<ment>the same way</ment>). It may also indicate salient characteristics of the #Lessor that affect the action (<ment>presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully</ment>).
<ex>Stuart <fex name="Manner">reluctantly</fex> <t>rented</t> her his last apartment .</ex>

name: Duration
description:
The length of time that the #Goods are (or have been) in the #Lessee s possession.   

<ex>Our neighbors have <t>rented</t> their house out <fex name="Duration">for the last ten years</fex>.</ex>

name: Speaker
description: The #Speaker is the person who communicates their cessation of association with the #Content.

name: Content
description:
The idea that the #Speaker no longer associates with, whether it be an activity, a propostion, or a group.

<ex>Bonnie Charlie then <t>renounced</t> <fex name="con">his aspirations to the throne</fex>.</ex>

name: Medium
description:
#Medium is the physical entity or channel used by the #Speaker to transmit the statement of renunciation.

<ex>Castro <t>renounced</t> all involvement with the group <fex name="med">over the radio</fex>.</ex>

name: Addressee
description: The #Addressee is the person to whom the #Speaker is communicating. When this FE is expressed, it often appears in a prepositional phrase introduced by "to", or as a direct object.

name: Manner
description:
Any description of the renunciation which is not covered by more specific FEs, including secondary effects (<ment>quietly, loudly</ment>), and general descriptions comparing events (<ment>the same way</ment>). In addition, it may indicate salient characteristics of an #Speaker that also affect the action (<ment>presumptuously, coldly, deliberately, eagerly, carefully</ment>). 

<ex>Your government then <fex name="man">coldly and completely</fex> <t>renounced</t> you.</ex>

name: Means
description: An act whereby the #Speaker makes a statement of renunciation.

name: Time
description: The time when the statement is made.

name: Place
description: The FE specifies the place where the statement takes place.

name: Agent
description: Agent

name: Artifact
description: Artifact

name: Description
description: Description

name: Place
description: Place

name: Manner
description: Manner

name: Mean
description: Mean

name: Time
description: Time

name: Injured_party
description: The #Injured_party was affected by some previous wrongful act of the #Wrongdoer and who should benefit from the reparations.

name: Injury
description: The #Injury is the injurious action that  the #Wrongdoer committed against the #Injured_party.  Although conceptually present, this FE need not always be realized.

name: Wrongdoer
description: The #Wrongdoer makes amends to the #Injured_party for the #Injury.

name: Benefiting_action
description: The #Benefiting_action is performed by the #Wrongdoer to make amends.

name: Gift
description: It identifies the entity that the #Wrongdoer gives to the #Injured_party.

name: Manner
description: It marks any description of the #Wrongdoer's making of reparations not covered by more specific FEs, including epistemic modification, (e.g. probably), and general descriptions comparing events (e.g. the same way). It may indicate salient characteristics of a #Wrongdoer that also affect the action ( e.g. presumptuously).

name: Place
description: It identifies the #Place where the #Wrongdoer makes amends.

name: Time
description: It identifies the #Time when amends are made.

name: Purpose
description: It identifies the #Purpose for which the #Wrongdoer makes restitution to the #Injured_party.

name: Degree
description: The extent to which the #Wrongdoer makes amends for the #Injury.

name: Loaner
description:
The person who is owed #Money by the #Debtor.

<ex>If you borrow, you ll have to <t>repay</t> <fex name="Loaner">the bank</fex></ex>

name: Debtor
description:
The person who is giving #Money to the #Loaner to return an earlier borrowed amount.

<ex><fex name="Debtor">I</fex> <t>paid</t> Johnson <t>back</t> for the movie ticket.</ex>

name: Money
description:
The amount of #Money that the #Debtor gives to the #Loaner, which may or may not be the full amount of the #Loan.

<ex><t>Pay</t> me ba<t></t>ck <fex name="Money">my 20 dollars</fex>!</ex>

name: Loan
description:
A situation or a representative of a situation prior to repayment in which the #Debtor uses the #Loaner s money.

<ex>You must <t>repay</t> <fex name="Loan">the mortgage</fex> in full.</ex>

name: Degree
description:
The level to which the Loan can be considered repaid in its entirety.

<ex>He wishes to <fex name="Degree">completely</fex> <t>repay</t> the loan.</ex>

name: Time
description:
When the #Debtor gives the #Loaner money.

<ex>Don t worry, I ll <t>pay</t> you <t>back</t> <fex name="Time">later</fex>.</ex>

name: Manner
description:
How the #Debtor gives #Money to the #Loaner.

<ex>Paul <fex name="Manner">quietly</fex> <t>repaid</t> all of his debts and skipped town.</ex>

name: Place
description:
The physical location where the repayment takes place.

<ex>They <t>repaid</t> Lenny <fex name="Place">in a dark alleyway</fex>.</ex>

name: Means
description:
What the <fex name="">Debtor</fex> does in order to repay the <fex name="">Loaner</fex>.

<ex>I <t>repaid</t> my loan by mowing their lawn once a week.</ex>

name: Enemy
description:
The  <fex name="Enemy">Enemy</fex> is the entity that fails to overtake the #Defender.

<ex>General Monk would <t>repel</t> <fex name="Enemy">Jacobite invaders</fex>.</ex>

name: Defender
description:
The #Defender is the land, government, and its people that the #Enemy fails to seize.

<ex><fex name="Defender">General Winter</fex> <t>repelled</t> invaders from Napoleon to Hitler.</ex>

name: Invasion_act
description:
This FE refers to the #Invasion_act perpetrated by the #Enemy.

<ex><fex name="Defender">Spain</fex> pledged to <t>repel</t> <fex name="Invasion_act">any Allied invasion</fex>.</ex>

name: Degree
description: This FE describes the intensity and extent of the repelling event.

name: Manner
description: The manner in which the #Defender repels the #Enemy.

name: Means
description: The action by which the repelling event is made possible through the efforts of the #Defender.

name: Place
description: The location where the #Defender repels the #Enemy.

name: Time
description: The #Time when the #Defender succeeds to repel the #Enemy.

name: Instrument
description: The tool used by the #Defender to repel the #Enemy.

name: Agent
description: The conscious entity, generally a person, that performs actions resulting in the #New entity occupying the position.

name: New
description:
The #New entity is the person or thing that the #Agent sets to fill a #Role.

<ex>I don t know; perhaps you could <t>substitute</t> <fex name="New">regular sugar</fex>.</ex>

name: Old
description:
The entity that formerly occupied the position.

<ex>If you <t>substitute</t> a 15" arm <fex name="old">for the 50cm one</fex>, it works pretty well.</ex>
<ex>Please <t>replace</t> <fex name="old">the broken fixture</fex> as soon as possible.</ex>

name: Means
description: An intentional action performed by the #Agent that accomplishes the replacement.

name: Place
description: This FE identifies where the #Agent installs the #New entity.

name: Purpose
description: This FE identifies the purpose for which an #Agent places the #New entity in a position.

name: Time
description: This FE identifies the time when the #Agent installs the New entity.

name: Manner
description: Any description of the event which is not covered by more specific FEs, including epistemic modification  secondary effects (<ment>quietly</ment>, <ment>loudly</ment>), and general descriptions comparing events (<ment>the same way</ment>). It may also indicate salient characteristics or states of an #Agent that also affect the action (<ment>presumptuously</ment>, <ment>coldly</ment>, <ment>deliberately</ment>, <ment>eagerly</ment>, <ment>carefully</ment>).

name: Role
description:
The category that the #New and #Old entities fit into as a result of the function they serve.

<ex>AN AUSTRALIAN state has proposed the country adopt a bill of rights and <t>replace</t> the Queen <fex name="rol">as head of state</fex> with an Australian president .</ex>

name: Informer
description: The #Informer reports the activities of the #Wrongdoer to the #Authorities.

name: Wrongdoer
description: The #Wrongdoer has his or her activities reported to the #Authorities.

name: Authorities
description: The #Authorities are the officials, government or otherwise, who handle misbehavior.

name: Behavior
description: The #Behavior is the action or actions for which the #Wrongdoer is reported.

name: Time
description: The #Time when the #Informer reports the #Behavior of the #Wrongdoer.

name: Place
description: The #Place is the location where the reporting occurs.

name: Representative
description: The #Representative speaks for another.

name: Origin
description: The #Origin is the place where the #Representative was born or lived a salient part of her or his life.

name: Age
description: The #Age is the length of time the #Representative has been alive.

name: Ethnicity
description: The #Ethnicity is the religious, racial, national, socio-economic or cultural group to which the #Representative belongs.

name: Rank
description: #Rank is the position in a hierarchy that a #Representative occupies within an organization.

name: Place_of_employment
description: This FE identifies the Place where the #Representative works.

name: Represented
description: The #Represented authorizes the #Representative to communicate on its behalf.

name: Type
description: This FE identifies the #Type of #Representative.

name: Compensation
description: The #Compensation is the payment that the #Representative receives for performing a task.

name: Contract_basis
description:
The #Contract_basis describes the conditions of employment with respect to permanency, hours per time period, or payment basis.

<ex>Smithers is a <fex name="cb">half-time</fex> <t>spokesperson</t> for Procteur et Gamble </ex>

name: Phenomenon
description: The event, idea, or emotion evoked by the #Entity.

name: Entity
description: An object that has some association with a #Phenomenon.

name: Degree
description:
This FE describes the #Degree to which the #Entity represents the #Phenomenon.

<ex><fex name="Entity">That mural</fex> <t>is</t> <fex name="Degree">hardly</fex> <t>a symbol</t> <fex name="Phenomenon">of what we re all about!</fex></ex>

name: Time
description: The time period in which the representing occurs.

name: Means
description:
This FE describes the means through which the representing occurs.
<ex><fex name="Phenomenon">What</fex> does <fex name="Entity">a black and white snake</fex> <t>symbolize</t> <fex name="Means">in a dream</fex>?</ex>

name: Cognizer
description: The #Cognizer is the person or group of people who believe in the representation.

name: Place
description: The #Place where the representing event occurs.

name: Speaker
description:
The #Speaker, as in other communication frames, is generally an External Argument. 

<ex><fex name="Speaker">The judge</fex> <t>ordered</t> the plaintiff to return the money.</ex>

name: Addressee
description:
In this frame the #Addressee can either occur as an NP Object (usually with a following complement clause) or as a PP Complement of verbs or nouns.

<ex>The child <t>implored</t> <fex name="Addressee">her parents</fex> to let her stay up late.</ex>
<ex>I <t>pleaded</t> <fex name="Addressee">with the bus driver</fex> to let me on without paying.</ex>
<ex>I made an <t>appeal</t> <fex name="Addressee">to the passers-by</fex> to help me.</ex>

name: Message
description:
The #Message is the content of the request that can be expressed as a quote, in a finite or non-finite clausal Complement, an NP Object or PP Complement.
<ex><fex name="Message">"I d like an extension,"</fex> <t>begged</t> Pat.</ex>
<ex>The directors <t>requested</t> <fex name="Message">that Pat resign</fex>.</ex>
<ex>Pat <t>begged</t> them <fex name="Message">to reconsider</fex>.</ex>
<ex>The students <t>requested</t> <fex name="Message">an extension of the deadline</fex>.</ex>
<ex>The offender <t>begged</t> <fex name="Message">for clemency</fex>.</ex>

name: Medium
description:
The #Medium is the physical entity or channel used to transmit the #Message. The #Medium is frequently expressed as a PP Complement headed by <ment>on</ment> or <ment>in</ment>. 
<ex>The parents of the missing child made an <t>appeal</t> <fex name="Medium">on television</fex>.</ex>
<ex>The parents of the missing child made an <t>appeal</t> <fex name="Medium">in the papers</fex>.</ex>

name: Manner
description:
#Manner indicates the manner in which the #Message is delivered.

<ex>Don t stop, " he <t>commanded</t> <fex name="Manr">huskily</fex>.</ex>
<ex>"Maybe that was the basic problem," she <t>told</t> him <fex name="Manr">wearily</fex>.</ex>

name: Means
description:
An act performed by the #Speaker that results in a request being made and received.

<ex>He <t>asked</t> for food <fex name="Mns">by holding his hand out with a doleful expression</fex>.</ex>

name: Time
description:
The interval during which the #Speaker makes the request.

<ex><fex name="Speaker">I</fex> <t>urged</t> <fex name="Add">him</fex> <fex name="Message">to concede</fex> <fex name="Time">yesterday</fex>.</ex>

name: Customer
description:
The #Customer is the person or business ordering the #Entity from the #Supplier.

<ex><fex name="Cust">Simone</fex> <t>ordered</t> a hamburger and fries.</ex>

name: Entity
description:
The object ordered by the #Customer.

<ex>Liz <t>ordered</t> <fex name="Ent">a new espresso maker</fex> for the office.</ex>

<ex>His <t><fex name="Ent">order</fex></t> did not arrive on time.</ex>

name: Supplier
description:
The person or organization from whom the #Customer orders the #Entity.

<ex>Mrs. Apfelbaum <t>ordered</t> a coffee <fex name="S">from the barista</fex>.</ex>

name: Medium
description:
The channel used by the #Customer to transmit the request.

<ex>Harold <t>ordered</t> the material <fex name="Med">via email</fex>.</ex>

name: Time
description:
The #Time at which the #Customer requested the #Entity.

<ex>Sara placed the <t>order</t> <fex name="Time">three days ago</fex>. </ex>

name: Manner
description:
The Manner in which the request is placed.

<ex>Mr. Shaw <fex name="Man">hastily</fex> <t>ordered</t> the first thing he saw on the menu.</ex>

name: Means
description:
An action performed by the #Customer that results in a request being made.

<ex>Cheri accidently <t>ordered</t> the jacket <fex name="Means">by clicking on the link</fex>.</ex>

name: Required_situation
description: The state-of-affairs which prevents #Negative_consequences.

name: Negative_consequences
description: An event or state of affairs that results if the required #Required_situation does not occur and which impacts some deprofiled entity in a way that the speaker finds undesirable.

name: Purpose
description: The event or state of affairs that is achieved by bringing about the #Required_situation and which entails the non-occurrance of #Negative_consequences.

name: Time
description:
The period during which the #Required_situation is necessary to prevent negative consequences.

<ex>He was told that <fex name="tim">when he got back</fex> , his column <t>needed</t> a serious rethink .</ex>

name: Place
description:
The location within which the #Required_situation is required.

<ex><fex name="pla">In California</fex> , seatbelts <t>must</t> be fastened at all times .</ex>

name: Degree
description:
The degree to which the #Required_situation is necessary.  The #Degree may either imply a modification of the direness of the #Negative_consequences, the probability of the #Negative_consequences (if the #Required_situation is not made to hold), or both.

<ex>He <fex name="deg">absolutely</fex> <t>must</t> be replaced .</ex>

name: Manner
description: Manner

name: Dependent
description: The state-of-affairs which cannot hold without the #Requirement.

name: Requirement
description: The state-of-affairs that must be present or obtain in order for the #Dependent to obtain.

name: Required_entity
description: An entity that has to be present for the #Dependent state of affairs to obtain

name: Degree
description: The extent to which the #Dependent depends on the presence of the #Requirement.

name: Time
description: The time at which the #Requirement is necessary for the #Dependent.

name: Place
description: #Place denotes a location in which the specified relation between #Requirement and #Dependent holds.

name: Dependent_individual
description: The individual in whose interest it is to have the #Required_entity or for the #Requirement state of affairs to obtain.

name: Required_individual
description: An individual that has to provide the #Required_entity or see to it that the #Requirement state of affairs holds.

name: Agent
description:
The sentient being who rescues the #Patient or #Asset from the #Harmful_situation.

<ex><fex name="Agent">He</fex> <t>rescued</t> the innocent girl from a fate worse than death. </ex>

name: Patient
description:
The living being who is rescued by the #Agent.

<ex><fex name="Patient">Three foxes</fex> were <t>saved</t> from being savagely ripped apart.</ex>

name: Asset
description:
The entity that is judged to be valueable which might be lost or damaged by the #Harmful_situation.

<ex>She <t>saved</t> <fex name="Asset">countless lives</fex> during her short stay on the plantation.</ex>

name: Harmful_situation
description:
An event that occurs which could result in injury or harm to the #Patient or #Asset.

<ex>Three foxes were <t>saved</t> <fex name="Harm">from being savagely ripped apart</fex>.</ex>

name: Time
description:
The time at which the #Agent rescues the #Patient or #Asset.

<ex>She <t>saved</t> my life on the bridge <fex name="Time">yesterday</fex>.</ex>

name: Manner
description:
The way in which the #Agent rescues the #Patient or #Asset.

<ex>Kevin informed her that he would <fex name="Manner">happily</fex> <t>save</t> her, given the opportunity.</ex>

name: Place
description:
The location at which the #Agent rescues the #Patient or #Asset.

<ex>She <t>saved</t> my life <fex name="Place">on the bridge</fex> yesterday.</ex>

name: Instrument
description:
The tool with which the #Agent saves the #Patient or #Asset.

<ex>She <t>saved</t> my life <fex name="Instrument">with her words</fex>.</ex>

name: Means
description:
The means by which the #Agent rescues the #Patient or #Asset.

<ex>The nurse <t>saved</t> my life <fex name="Means">by performing CPR</fex>.</ex>

name: Purpose
description:
The purpose for which the #Agent rescues the #Patient or #Asset.

<ex>You know he only <t>saved</t> the dog <fex name="Purpose">to get close to Miranda</fex>.</ex>

name: Degree
description:
The extent to which the #Patient or #Asset is actually saved.

<ex>He <fex name="Degree">wholly</fex> <t>saved</t> the town from hunger.</ex>

name: Researcher
description:
The people engaged in a research endeavor.

<ex>Before he became an astronaut, <fex name="res">Grunsfeld</fex> <m>did</m> <t>research</t> in physics. </ex>

name: Question
description:
A significant unanswered open proposition which the #Researcher is attempting to resolve.

<ex>When we have <t>researched</t> <fex name="que">why Brazilian mothers have stopped breastfeeding</fex>, principal reasons given are  poor milk  and  insufficient milk  .</ex>

name: Topic
description:
A phenomenon or idea involved in a #Question.

<ex>Would you take part in <t>research</t> <fex name="top">on the best place to have a baby</fex>? </ex>

name: Field
description:
An area of inquiry.

<ex></ex>

name: Time
description:
The time when  the research is carried out.
